column1: # Authoritative prose + formulas: docs/model_loop_spec.md §4. This file is the
# MACHINE-READABLE order the loop walks. A candidate is "done" iff run_log.csv
# has a row whose model_file column contains its id (e.g. "...fit_candidate_C2...").
# The loop fits the LOWEST-numbered id with no run_log row. STOP when none remain

column3: STRUCTURAL DIAGNOSTIC (2026-06-14): m3 minus (1|trip_id), default adapt_delta — is the ~5,500-level trip RE (funnel) what breaks m3 convergence + a clean C0 reference

CONVERGENCE EXPERIMENT (2026-06-15): m3 with nu FIXED at 4 (constant prior). Localization showed nu railed at the lb=2 floor in 100% of draws of both m3 and C0_notrip — near-Cauchy likelihood is the suspected cause. HARD STOP if not clean.
baseline m3 at adapt_delta=0.99 — clean-converging reference (G2: zero divergences)
settled-rows-only TRAIN — does b_prev fall well below 1 (feed carry-forward validity probe)
+ log(headway_obs/headway_sched) — bunching amplifies delay beyond carry-forward
+ timepoint*previous_stop_delay — propagation resets at scheduled timepoints
s(shape_dist_traveled) replaces linear — non-linear delay accumulation along route
+ (1+previous_stop_delay|route_id) random slopes — propagation strength varies by route
+ ar(stop_sequence, gr=trip_serv_id, p=1) — residual serial correlation along trip
+ precip_mm + temp_c (ECCC) — weather adds signal beyond schedule features
top-50 routes x 2000 rows — subsampling, not structure, is the binding constraint
sigma ~ is_rush_hour + s(hour) — time-varying residual scale (calibration gains)
